payload.title: Canonical Inbox submission friction from cross-shell observation report
payload.summary: Submitting inbox observations from a Windows PowerShell -> WSL -> Narada path exposed repeated friction in enum discoverability, invalid-value diagnostics, noninteractive WSL PATH, and cross-shell JSON/file quoting. One attempted correction even submitted an empty payload envelope, showing that failure can look superficially successful.

observed_friction.detail: Initial attempt used source kind chat and authority level operator_report, but valid values required user_chat and one of none, user_statement, operator_confirmed, system_observed, agent_reported.

observed_friction.issue: Help output did not show allowed enum values
observed_friction.detail: narada inbox submit --help listed flags but not accepted values. Source inspection was needed to find parseSourceKind and parseAuthorityLevel.

observed_friction.issue: PowerShell to WSL to Bash quoting was fragile
observed_friction.detail: The first heredoc attempt let backticks inside JSON strings be interpreted by the shell, producing bogus command-not-found errors.

observed_friction.issue: Inline JSON is risky across shell boundaries
observed_friction.detail: Even with --payload-stdin, nested PowerShell/WSL/Bash made quoting easy to get wrong. A payload file is more reliable.
The CLI returned Missing or invalid --source-kind, --source-ref, --kind, or --authority-level without naming the invalid field or allowed values.

observed_friction.issue: Noninteractive WSL PATH did not include narada
observed_friction.detail: The noninteractive WSL shell could not find narada, requiring the absolute path /home/andrey/.local/bin/narada.

observed_friction.issue: PowerShell command substitution collided with Bash command substitution
observed_friction.detail: An attempt to use mktemp inside a double-quoted PowerShell command caused PowerShell to evaluate $(mktemp) before WSL saw it.

observed_friction.issue: Malformed temp-file quoting produced a successful empty-payload envelope
observed_friction.detail: Envelope env_b384b82a-dc77-4253-8190-70089c85bbfd was submitted with payload {} while trying to submit this observation, demonstrating that submit success does not necessarily mean payload admission matched operator intent.

proposed_improvements: Show valid enum values in narada inbox submit --help.
On invalid enum, report the exact invalid field and allowed values.
Document PowerShell/WSL-safe submission patterns, especially payload-file as the preferred cross-shell path.
Consider a first-class narada inbox submit-observation wrapper for common observation reports with --title, --summary, and repeated --proposed-action flags.
Ensure noninteractive shell PATH includes ~/.local/bin, or document absolute CLI path usage.
Add an ergonomics note: for cross-shell submission, write JSON to a temp file and pass --payload-file rather than using heredoc or inline JSON.
Consider warning or confirmation when submitting observation/task_candidate envelopes with empty object payloads.

suggested_target.reason: The inbox is intended as a routine intake surface; submission should not require source inspection or fragile shell quoting, and successful empty-payload admission can hide operator-intent loss.

promotion.note: Envelope archived; no target-zone mutation was performed.
